codeReview.summary: The security-cli-serve feature implementation fully addresses the path traversal vulnerability and CORS security requirements. The implementation in cli/src/commands/serve.rs correctly canonicalizes paths and verifies they remain within the served directory. The CORS layer is only added when --cors-allow-origin is explicitly specified, defaulting to same-origin behavior. The test suite includes 12 comprehensive security tests that verify path traversal protection and CORS behavior using raw TCP sockets to bypass HTTP client path normalization.

summary: Security fix for CLI serve command successfully implemented and tested. Path traversal attempts are blocked with 403 Forbidden, and CORS defaults to same-origin only unless explicitly configured via --cors-allow-origin flag. All 84 tests pass including 12 new security-specific tests.
